title: Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Violates Sanctions Near Denmark
summary: Russian tanker SIENNA maneuvers through Great Belt Strait despite sanctions. Danish authorities monitor vessel closely. Incident highlights ongoing shadow fleet operations evading Western restrictions.
contents:
## Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Violates Sanctions Near Denmark

### Overview
Russian tanker SIENNA maneuvers through Great Belt Strait despite sanctions. Danish authorities monitor vessel closely. Incident highlights ongoing shadow fleet operations evading Western restrictions.

### Event Details
Ray Funseth reports Russian-flagged tanker SIENNA transits Great Belt Strait near Denmark, evading EU and G7 sanctions on Russian oil transport. Vessel operates within shadow fleet, switches off AIS transponder intermittently to obscure movements. Danish Navy deploys patrol vessels to shadow SIENNA during passage. Funseth analyzes tanker patterns, reveals frequent dark fleet maneuvers in Baltic Sea to deliver sanctioned crude to global markets. United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) issues advisory on increased shadow fleet activity. Funseth shares satellite imagery showing SIENNA alongside other sanctioned vessels in Kaliningrad earlier week. Analysts track similar tankers rerouting through Danish straits to bypass stricter monitoring in North Sea. Russia continues expanding shadow fleet with over 600 vessels, transfers oil via ship-to-ship operations off Greece and Malta. United States Treasury designates additional tankers this month for sanctions evasion. Funseth highlights SIENNA's history of insurance lapses and flag-hopping from Liberia to Russia. Incident underscores challenges in enforcing price cap on Russian oil exports. NATO enhances maritime surveillance in response to heightened Russian naval presence. Funseth urges stricter AIS enforcement and satellite tracking to disrupt evasion tactics. Shadow fleet transports 70% of Russia's seaborne oil, sustains war economy despite Western measures.

### Source Context
@RayFunseth (Ray Funseth) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on maritime osint vessel tracking sanctions iran russia tanker pattern analysis.
